title: Digital platforms implement new regulations and artificial intelligence features
summary: The United Kingdom bans social media for minors under 16, Discord tests age verification, HBO Max uses AI for advertisements, and Apple adds AI features to iOS.

title: Technological competition between China and the United States intensifies on several fronts
summary: China launches an AI model, dominates in robotaxis, publishes a manual for attacking aircraft carriers, and prepares orbital data centers, while restricting critical materials to the US.

title: Debates and news in the artificial intelligence industry
summary: Executives like Nadella, LeCun, and Altman discuss the future of AI, while Anthropic faces regulation, Claude adds identity verification, and AWS launches services for AI agents.

title: Artificial intelligence in education raises concerns and restrictions
summary: Norway bans AI in primary school, a study indicates it does more harm than good, and it is found to inflate grades by promoting outsourced work.

title: Growing rejection of artificial intelligence in society and businesses
summary: Americans turn against AI, companies limit its use due to costs, dating apps reject it, and Tim Ferriss says AI is killing his books.

title: News in the video game industry: from Half-Life 3 to a free strategy game
summary: Valve hints at Half-Life 3 with a message in Dota 2, the Oliver Kahn lawsuit against EA is recalled, and Beyond All Reason, a free RTS inspired by Total Annihilation, is released.

title: Labor and economic trends: automation, bonuses, and changes in work
summary: GM lays off workers and adds robots, Meta acknowledges low morale, AI creates two-speed labor, bonuses in South Korea generate inflation, and more fathers become primary caregivers.

title: AI and entertainment: from concerts with AirPods to psychological principles in football
summary: Concert attendees use AirPods as earplugs, soccer players apply psychological principles, and actors distance themselves from Peter Thiel at a conference.

title: Polymarket accused of paying for misleading videos about fake bets
summary: The prediction platform Polymarket allegedly paid creators to spread fake videos of winning bets to boost its growth.

title: The high cost of memory affects smartphone manufacturers like Nothing and Apple
summary: Nothing cancels new CMF smartphones due to memory costs, while Apple indicates it will raise prices for the same reason.

title: Governments and technology: surveillance, leaks, and military defense with AI
summary: Kansas City installs facial recognition on buses, Canada spends millions on Palantir, Texas suffers a massive leak, and the US prepares anti-aircraft lasers using drone footage from Ukraine.

title: Experimentation with open-source and local artificial intelligence models
summary: Researchers test local models like Qwen and Apertus, Microsoft creates an LLM in Age of Empires, and the advantage of open models over proprietary ones is defended.

title: Hardware news: from durable graphics cards to new AI CPUs
summary: The RTX 4070 and 5090 show durability, Rokid glasses offer good navigation, Intel launches Raptor Lake with 24 cores and prepares ACE with AMD to accelerate AI on CPUs.

title: Advances and discoveries in health and science: from Moderna's approval to meteorites
summary: The FDA approves Moderna's vaccine, Ozempic reduces impulsivity, a meteorite is discovered at Russian Customs, and Earth receives radioactive rain from supernovae.

title: Business news: Airbnb, Tesla, SpaceX, Ubisoft, and Microsoft
summary: Airbnb rejects restrictions in Sydney, a Tesla on autopilot kills a woman, SpaceX receives low ESG rating, the co-founder of Ubisoft passes away, and Microsoft optimizes Windows search.

title: Reflections and analysis on technology, programming, and society
summary: Various articles cover topics from programming in 1992 to the ethics of big tech, including rent in New York, the truth about employment fraud, and the utility of AI models.

title: Offers and resources for downloading software and devices
summary: Safe websites for downloading apps are recommended, Office 2024 is offered at a low price, and Amazon device deals are highlighted before Prime Day.

title: Engineering stories: from a missing Boeing to a ship tunnel in Norway
summary: A Boeing 727 missing since 2001 remains a mystery, a Roman canal is discovered in Germany, a Chinese farmer builds submarines, and Norway approves the first maritime tunnel.

title: Natural and environmental curiosities in the world
summary: Singapore boasts about its drinking water, the human body loses appetite with extreme heat, beavers prevent floods in London, and Spain seeks pet-friendly beaches.

title: Automotive ingenuity of the past: the thermometer in the rearview mirror
summary: A historical invention allowed measuring the outside temperature from the car's rearview mirror at a time when having a thermometer was a luxury.
